expectations.text: Covers SEO items (title, meta, h1, canonical)
expectations.evidence: Mentions title tags, meta descriptions, heading structure, and canonical URLs in the SEO-Relevant Observations section listing the SEO guide contents.

Output does not list SEO-coded items (e.g., SEO-01, SEO-02) or any equivalent structured checklist. Instead provides a general narrative description of what the SEO guide covers without a systematic item listing.

No per-item results table or structured evaluation. The output gives a single overall conclusion ('No SEO issues were found because no HTML files exist') rather than evaluating each SEO item individually.

suggestions: Without the skill, the model produced a narrative-style report rather than a structured per-item SEO checklist. The skill provides the structured SEO-01 through SEO-10 framework that drives systematic evaluation.
eval_feedback.overall: The without-skill run for the English prompt lacked structured SEO-coded items and per-item results. It correctly identified no HTML files existed but did not provide the systematic checklist-based evaluation that the skill-guided runs produced.
